A Data-driven Hydrophobicity Scale for Predicting Liquid-Liquid Phase Separation of Proteins Thomas Dannenhoffer-Lafage and Robert B. Best∗ Laboratory of Chemical Physics, National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases, National Institutes of Health, Bethesda, MD 20892-0520 E-mail: robert.best2@nih.gov

S1

[Page 2]

Supporting Figures

Figure S1: Optimization error as a function of iteration. Error using Kyte-Doolittle hydrophobicities, or residue-averaged Kaptcha-Rossky hydrophobicities with = 0.1, are shown as blue and orange symbols respectively for reference.

S2

[Page 3]

Figure S2: Optimization error as a function of iteration using regularization with varying strength, as indicated in legend.

Figure S3: Validation error of original HPS model with Kapcha-Rossky hydrophobicities, and optimized HPS with and without regularization (see legend).

S3

[Page 4]

Figure S4: Off-diagonal optimization: optimization error where every pair-wise interaction was allowed vary independently, starting from the end of the optimization where interactions were generated from hydrophobicities via mixing rules. Allowing for the more than ten-fold increase in paramters (20 vs 210) this only marginally improves the optimization error.

Figure S5: Cross-validation error of the off-diagonal optimization. The cross-validation error of the off-diagonal optimisation is only marginally better than than the diagonal optimization.

S4
